metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a post-update proving command to AC2 — e.g. `grep -rn 'two green votes\|watch votes\|treat.*green.*land' docs/ CLAUDE.md CONTRIBUTING.md && echo 'STALE PATTERNS REMAIN' || echo 'CLEAN'` — so the executor can produce evidence of completeness, not just effort.
findings.finding: AC2 instructs the executor to grep the repo and update all deprecated-pattern occurrences, but provides no post-update verification command to confirm zero occurrences remain — the completeness of the sweep is claimed but unmeasured.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC2 ('Grep the whole repo…')
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC2: 'After updating, run `grep -rn --include="*.md" "two green votes\|watch votes\|treat.*green.*land" .` and confirm zero matches before marking done.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Require AC1 to mandate that the rewritten landing section either enumerates the typed outcome values with their per-outcome agent actions inline, or explicitly cross-references `docs/land-contract.md` (the stable contract defined by S4 AC5) as the decision table agents must consult.
findings.finding: AC1 instructs the rewritten docs to tell agents to 'act on the single typed outcome' but never requires the docs to enumerate the outcome values or the per-outcome decision rules. The outcome enum vocabulary (merged, needs_rebase, ci_failed, review_failed, abandoned, timed_out, error, pending) and what action each value demands (rebase vs fix CI vs fix review findings vs investigate) is defined in S4 / `docs/land-contract.md` but is not required to appear in or be cross-referenced from the rewritten CLAUDE.md/CONTRIBUTING.md landing section. This is an instruction-locality gap: the processing protocol is not co-located with the section that tells agents to use it.
findings.location: AC1 — `CLAUDE.md` + `CONTRIBUTING.md` landing section rewrite
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC1 to read: '…; act on the single typed outcome per the decision table in `docs/land-contract.md` (cross-link it inline); the rewritten section must enumerate or directly reference at minimum: merged → done; needs_rebase → rebase and re-push; ci_failed → inspect CI run; review_failed → address LLM-Review findings; timed_out/error → retry or escalate.'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either enumerate the outcome values in the plan (e.g. `LANDED | CONFLICT | REVIEW_REQUIRED | …`) or add an explicit pointer: 'outcome enum values as defined in S4's spec; ADR-0042 must reproduce them verbatim.'
findings.finding: AC3 requires the ADR to record 'the agent contract + outcome enum' but the plan never defines the enum values, nor points to where they are defined (e.g. in S4's spec), leaving the executor to invent or guess the enum.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC3 ('records the auto-lander, the agent contract + outcome enum…')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a description of the 'staged serial→batch plan' to the plan body (or a referenced document) so that AC3's requirement to record it in the ADR is backed by described, verifiable content.
findings.finding: AC3 asks the ADR to record 'the staged serial→batch plan', but no section of the plan body describes what this staged plan is, what 'serial' and 'batch' mean in context, or who owns it. This content is demanded by the criterion but has no described work backing it.
findings.location: AC3 vs. plan body (Why / What / Scope / Dependencies)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief description of the serial→batch staged plan to the plan body (or reference a specific existing document/PR that specifies it), so the ADR-0042 author has a settled design to record rather than one to invent.
findings.finding: The ADR-0042 AC requires it to record 'the staged serial→batch plan,' but the plan nowhere describes what that staged plan is. An implementer writing the ADR would need to invent the design details rather than document a pre-agreed decision, risking an ADR that is either incomplete or inconsistent with the actual design intent.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — `docs/adr/0042-*.md` bullet
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan body, add a 'Serial→batch staged plan' subsection that summarizes: (1) Phase 1 — serial single-PR landing, (2) Phase 2 — batched parallel landing, and the trigger/criteria for promotion between phases. Reference the ADR AC to this subsection.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan (or AC3) that ADR-0042 documents the outcome enum *by reference* to `docs/land-contract.md` as the normative source, rather than duplicating the enum table — so ADR-0042 records the design rationale while land-contract.md remains the single versioned enum definition.
findings.finding: The plan is silent on the relationship between ADR-0042's agent-contract/outcome-enum section and `docs/land-contract.md`, the 'stable, versioned contract' document S4 (brightish-growable-dodo) creates as the normative outcome-enum source. If ADR-0042 duplicates the enum table and exit-code table, the two documents can diverge; if ADR-0042 only references `docs/land-contract.md`, the implementer must know to do that without being told.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3: 'agent contract + outcome enum'; S4 AC: 'docs/land-contract.md … stable, versioned contract'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in AC3 whether ADR-0042 is the canonical enum definition (and `docs/land-contract.md` derives from it) or merely references `docs/land-contract.md` as the stable contract — add a one-line statement of precedence to resolve the ambiguity.
findings.finding: AC3 requires ADR-0042 to record 'the agent contract + outcome enum' while S4 AC5 separately establishes `docs/land-contract.md` as the stable, versioned, canonical source of the enum. The plan never specifies the relationship between these two documents — which is normative, whether ADR-0042 merely references `docs/land-contract.md` or re-states the enum independently — leaving an implementer unable to determine whether to duplicate, reference, or delegate the enum definition.
findings.location: AC3 — `docs/adr/0042-*.md` vs S4 AC5 `docs/land-contract.md`
findings.suggested_fix: Align AC3 with S4 AC5's language: change 'records the agent contract + outcome enum' to 'references the agent contract and outcome enum as defined in `docs/land-contract.md` (S4), and records the design rationale for the enum structure.' This makes docs/land-contract.md the single normative source.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the 'What' / 'Scope' sections to explicitly describe the repo-wide audit-and-replace work that AC2 requires, so the deliverable is not orphaned from its criterion.
findings.finding: AC2 requires grepping the whole repo and updating ALL occurrences of the deprecated 'two green votes / watch votes' pattern, but the 'What' section only describes rewriting 'this repo's landing section in CLAUDE.md + CONTRIBUTING.md' and 'Scope' says 'Docs + ADR only'. The broader multi-file audit is uncovered work in the plan body.
findings.location: AC2 vs. 'What' / 'Scope' sections
findings.suggested_fix: Add to 'What': 'Audit the entire repo for the deprecated two-green-votes / watch-votes pattern (e.g., docs/your-first-change.md) and update every occurrence to the new contract.' Update 'Scope' to 'Docs + ADR only (including all doc files repo-wide that reference the old landing contract).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an Acceptance Criterion (or explicit Definition of Done) that verifies S1 (Autosubmit label) and S4 (land command) are live before this branch may merge to main — converting the prose dependency into a checkable gate.
findings.finding: The Dependencies section states that docs 'must not merge to main until the label and command they instruct agents to use are actually live' (S1 and S4), but no Acceptance Criterion verifies this merge-ordering gate. If the gating condition fails silently, the deliverable could land pointing at non-existent tooling.
findings.location: Dependencies section vs. Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: '[ ] Merge is blocked (e.g., via branch protection or manual checklist) until S1 (`sterilized-papery-horse`) and S4 (`brightish-growable-dodo`) are confirmed live in the target environment.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC: 'Confirm S1 (`sterilized-papery-horse`, 843b-801f-9ef9-442d) and S4 (`brightish-growable-dodo`, 73f5-4df7-4fac-4738) are merged to main before this PR is merged.'
findings.finding: The merge-ordering constraint ('docs must not merge to main until the label and command they instruct agents to use are actually live') is stated only in the Dependencies prose section, not as a verifiable Acceptance Criterion. A reviewer approving the PR can easily miss this gate, allowing the docs to land while S1/S4 are still pending.
findings.location: Dependencies section + Acceptance Criteria (absence of a gate AC)
findings.suggested_fix: Add as the first AC bullet: '- [ ] **Gate**: S1 and S4 are confirmed merged; link their merge commits here before requesting final review.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `infra/runbooks/github-mirror-lock.md` to the file_impact list and AC2's grep-and-update target set, since it contains 'the gate — two votes' vote-watching language that must be updated to the new contract.
findings.finding: infra/runbooks/github-mirror-lock.md contains 'the gate — two votes' teaching language (line 234) that instructs contributors to monitor votes, yet is not named in the plan's edit set or AC2's grep-and-update scope, leaving the deprecated pattern live in a consumer file after this story lands.
findings.location: AC2 — 'Grep the whole repo … update ALL occurrences'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to file_impact: `{"path": "infra/runbooks/github-mirror-lock.md", "reason": "deprecated two-votes teaching language"}` and note in AC2: 'including infra/runbooks/github-mirror-lock.md (line 234 teaches the old gate model).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` as an update target in AC2 (or explicitly acknowledge it is intentionally out-of-scope for this story with a rationale), since it teaches the old two-vote-watching gate model.
findings.finding: docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md teaches the old two-vote gate model (lines 246–260) with vote-watching language ('Two independent votes gate submit … LLM-Review … Verified') but is not enumerated in the plan's file_impact or AC2's update scope.
findings.location: AC2 — 'Grep the whole repo … update ALL occurrences'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/your-first-change.md` to the ticket's `file_impact` list (`set_file_impact`) so next-batch can detect scheduling conflicts on that file.
findings.finding: The plan's file_impact list (recorded in the ticket store) omits docs/your-first-change.md despite AC2 explicitly naming it as an example grep target that must be updated, creating a mismatch between the AC and the scheduling metadata that next-batch uses for conflict detection.
findings.location: AC2; ticket file_impact field
findings.suggested_fix: Run `set_file_impact b825-a233-97d4-45c2 [{"path": "docs/your-first-change.md", "reason": "deprecated two-votes pattern update"}]` (appending to the existing list).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly note in the Dependencies section (or AC3) that `docs/land-contract.md` (created by S4) must exist before ADR-0042 can be finalized, or add a verify command that confirms the file is present at merge time.
findings.finding: docs/land-contract.md is a named artifact that S4 (brightish-growable-dodo AC5) requires to exist before this story's docs (CLAUDE.md, ADR-0042) can cross-reference it — but the plan does not enumerate it as a dependency-supplied file or verify its creation path, leaving a broken reference risk if S4 is not yet merged when S6 is implemented.
findings.location: AC1 ('run `land`'), AC3 ('ADR-0042 … references it'), Dependencies section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the fallback for the id-lookup in AC3 — e.g. 'if `1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984` does not resolve, record it as a placeholder `[idea id unconfirmed — follow up]` in ADR-0042 and open a separate task to resolve it.'
findings.finding: AC3 contains the instruction 'confirm this id resolves in the store' — a verify-whether placeholder — but states no fallback action if the id `1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984` does NOT resolve, leaving the executor with an unspecified decision.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC3 ('confirm this id resolves in the store')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback / back-out plan (e.g., 'revert this PR and restore the prior landing section') and state the trigger conditions under which it would be invoked, satisfying T4's reversibility requirement.
findings.finding: The plan removes the existing agent landing contract from docs (the 'two green votes' model) without providing an explicit rollback or back-out plan. T4 requires more than acknowledgement of the break — it requires a reversibility mechanism. If the S1/S4 tooling is later reverted or delayed, agents will have docs pointing at a contract with no working implementation and no documented path back.
findings.location: Why / Dependencies / (absent) rollback section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' section: 'If S1 or S4 are reverted after this doc lands, revert this PR (or its squash commit) and restore the prior CLAUDE.md/CONTRIBUTING.md landing section until tooling is re-deployed. The revert is safe because this PR is docs-only with no schema or data changes.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the failure action for the id-resolution check: e.g., 'if `1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984` does not resolve, block merge and file a follow-up to register the idea before ADR-0042 can land.'
findings.finding: The AC requires 'confirm this id resolves in the store' for idea `1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984` before anchoring ADR-0042 to it, but the plan gives no guidance on what to do if the id does NOT resolve — whether to block merge, use a placeholder, or escalate. This leaves an ambiguous failure path that may strand a broken reference in the ADR.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — `docs/adr/0042-*.md` bullet, parenthetical '(confirm this id resolves in the store)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the AC bullet: 'If the id does not resolve in the store, do not merge ADR-0042; open a task to register the idea and re-verify before landing.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete validation mechanism to the ACs — e.g., a dogfooding step ('walk an agent through the conflict scenario using the new docs and confirm it acts on the typed outcome without polling votes') or a before/after workflow comparison demonstrating the babysitting pattern no longer occurs.
findings.finding: The plan names a clear beneficiary (agents/contributors) and a specific problem (silent conflict-while-votes-green reintroduces babysitting) but provides no concrete validation mechanism proving that agents actually adopt and follow the new contract correctly — all ACs describe document content, not an observable improvement in agent behavior.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all four bullets)
findings.suggested_fix: Append an AC such as: 'Dogfood: simulate a PR that reaches conflict-while-votes-green; confirm an agent following only these updated docs takes exactly one typed-outcome action and does not poll vote status.' Alternatively, reference a companion integration test that exercises the full lander + outcome enum path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either (a) define the 'staged serial→batch plan' concretely in the plan body so ADR-0042 records a real decision, or (b) drop that sub-item from AC3 and instead have ADR-0042 note that batch landing is deferred to a future design (pointing at the relevant idea ticket), consistent with how `1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984` defers the provider-seam.
findings.finding: AC3 requires ADR-0042 to record 'the staged serial→batch plan,' but no such plan is defined anywhere in S6, S4, S1, or the parent epic. The ADR author must invent a multi-phase architecture rather than record an existing decision — this is premature design documentation masquerading as an ADR.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'docs/adr/0042-*.md records … the staged serial→batch plan'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'the staged serial→batch plan' in AC3 with 'a note that serial landing is the current mode and batch landing is deferred to a future design phase (link to the relevant idea or epic when it exists).' This keeps the ADR honest about what has actually been decided.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief description of the 'staged serial→batch plan' (what 'serial' and 'batch' mean in the auto-lander context, and what the staging sequence is) to the plan's What or Scope section so the implementer has source material to record in ADR-0042.
findings.finding: AC3 requires ADR-0042 to record 'the staged serial→batch plan,' but nowhere in the plan is this staged plan described or cited. An implementer writing the ADR has no source material and must invent the content.
findings.location: AC3 — 'docs/adr/0042-*.md records … the staged serial→batch plan'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'staged serial→batch plan' in the plan body (or in the parent epic) so ADR-0042 can record it accurately — either describe the two phases (serial single-change landing today; batch/queue as a future extension) or remove that sub-requirement from AC3 if no such staged plan exists.
findings.finding: AC3 requires ADR-0042 to record 'the staged serial→batch plan,' but the plan body (Why/What/Scope/Dependencies) never defines or describes what that plan is. No upstream story (S1, S4) specifies a serial→batch evolution path either. An implementing agent must fabricate the architectural content of a mandatory ADR section, producing either a blank, a guess, or a hallucinated roadmap.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3: 'the staged serial→batch plan'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief 'Staged plan' subsection to 'What': 'Phase 1 (this epic): serial — the bot processes one `Autosubmit` change at a time, submitting it before picking up the next. Phase 2 (future, not in scope): batch/queue — process N changes in parallel using speculative merges; deferred until the rebase-treadmill cost justifies the complexity.' AC3 then accurately points at a defined plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence rationale in 'What' or 'Why' stating that ADR-0042 is a new ADR (not an amendment) because the auto-lander is an independent decision with its own security invariants, agent contract, and deployment boundary — distinct from FFO's submit-type decision.
findings.finding: The plan states no positive rationale for why ADR-0042 is authored as a new ADR rather than as an amendment to ADR-0040 (FFO) or ADR-0041 (carry), even though AC4 explicitly frames the auto-lander as sitting 'on top of' those two ADRs as its gate substrate.
findings.location: Why / What sections; Acceptance Criteria AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Add to 'What': 'ADR-0042 is a new ADR (not an amendment to ADR-0040/0041) because the auto-lander is a new service-layer decision with independent security invariants and agent-contract commitments, not a refinement of FFO's submit-type or vote-carry semantics.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define what 'the staged serial→batch plan' means (e.g., serial=one-at-a-time per stack now; batch=multi-stack parallel later) in the plan body or a referenced source so the ADR-0042 author has concrete content to record rather than having to invent it.
findings.finding: AC3 requires ADR-0042 to record 'the staged serial→batch plan,' but this term is defined nowhere in S6, S4, S1, the S3 story, or any fetched dependency. An implementer writing ADR-0042 must invent the content of this architectural section without any source material — it is a referenced protocol whose vocabulary is entirely unspecified.
findings.location: AC3 — `docs/adr/0042-*.md` content requirements
findings.suggested_fix: Add a subsection to the plan's 'What' or 'Scope' that describes the staged evolution (e.g., 'Phase 1: serial — one stack processed at a time; Phase 2: batch — concurrent multi-stack processing deferred until throughput demand justifies it'). This gives the ADR author a design decision to record rather than requiring invention.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
